preamble: The shift from the rhotic consonant [r] to the near-open front vowel [æ] is a rare form of vocalic lowering found in specific non-rhotic dialects. It typically occurs when a vocalized R (schwa) is pulled toward the front and bottom of the vowel space during systemic dialectal shifts.

examples.note: Traditional non-rhotic 'Plantation' accents often lowered the vocalized R to a quality approaching [æ].
